experiences.intentions: Key state: 64 is at row2 col3, with many empties on the right column. Bottom row has [8,2,2,0], so moving left immediately merges the two 2s into 4 while preserving empty space; other rows mostly compress without merges. Goal is to improve board consolidation and create safe merges without disrupting the high tile structure. Candidate actions: left gives an immediate merge and keeps the board compact; right also merges bottom 2s but shifts the 64 away from its current edge alignment; up creates a merge in column 1 but pulls smaller tiles upward and can clutter the top; down makes no useful merge and scatters larger tiles. Best sub-goal now is consolidate left with a guaranteed merge and maintain control.

A strong opportunity is on row 3 where the two 4s at the end can merge if moved right; row 2 also can shift right to place 32 and 64 together on the right edge. Goal is to improve board consolidation and create merges while avoiding scattering the high tile. Checking moves: left makes row2 [128,16,4,0], row3 [16,4,0,0], row4 [2,16,8,0] with one small merge in row2 from the two 2s; right also merges the 2s but shifts the 128 away from the left edge. Best sub-goal is keep the 128 anchored on the left while taking the available merge and preserving empties. Therefore choose left.

No immediate large merges available except the two 4s in column 3 can merge if moved up. Goal: improve board while preserving mobility and ideally keep the 128 anchored on the left side. Candidate actions: up merges the two 4s in the third column and keeps the 128 in the left column, creating a stronger top-heavy structure; down does not create a merge and shifts big tiles away; left creates no merge and may clutter rows; right also creates no merge and moves the 128 off the edge anchor. Best sub-goal now is take the available merge while maintaining structure, so choose up.

Merges available: moving left merges the two 16s on the third row into 32; moving right also merges those 16s. Up/down create less immediate value and may disturb the strong 128 placement. There are 5 empty spaces, mostly on the right side, so preserving space is important. Goal: keep the board stable while creating merges and avoiding moves that break structure or shift the 128 away unnecessarily. Candidate actions: Up would merge the top row 4+4 into 8 and keep the 128 in the left area, though it fills one space. Down would move tiles downward and may disturb the strong upper-left setup. Left would preserve the 128 on the left and compress rows, creating the merge on row 3 (2+4 no) and row 2 no merge; limited gain. Right would pull the 128 off the wall, which is usually bad. Best sub-goal is to maintain the high tile anchored while taking an available merge. So choose up.

Candidate moves: Left gives immediate merge on top row (2+0+0+0 unchanged) and second row compresses to [8,4,0,0], third row unchanged, fourth becomes [2,16,8,0], creating only one new empty and not much consolidation. Right makes row1 [0,0,0,2], row2 [0,0,8,4], row3 unchanged, row4 [0,2,16,8], also no merge. Up seems poor: columns mostly stack without merges and may pull small tiles above big ones. Down is best because column 4 merges 4+4 into 8 at bottom, while preserving/opening upper spaces and keeping larger values lower on the board. Sub-goal now: gain a merge and maintain lower-heavy structure. Therefore choose down.
experiences.tasks: Achieve the highest possible tile in 2048 by merging tiles strategically.

Candidate actions: left gives immediate merge on row4 (2+2 -> 4) and compresses all rows left, maintaining empties and improving structure; right also merges row4 but shifts big tiles away from left anchor; up creates no merges and reduces flexibility; down creates no immediate merges and scatters structure. Best sub-goal now is improve board monotonicity and gain a merge while keeping space.

experiences.intentions: Key state: top row is [4,8,8,2] with an immediate merge opportunity on the two 8s if moved left or right; there are 3 empty spaces mostly in lower rows. Highest tile 64 is at row 2 col 1, and the board is fairly cluttered, so preserving space while gaining a safe merge is important. Goal: increase tile value and maintain/open space without disrupting structure too much. Candidate actions: left merges the 8+8 into 16 on the top row and also compacts lower rows, creating/opening space; right also merges the 8s but shifts the top row toward the right and moves the bottom 2s into the right side, which is less favorable for keeping stronger tiles anchored left. Up produces no obvious merge and compresses into a tighter board. Down creates a 2+2 merge in the last column but drops the 64 downward and does not capitalize on the stronger 8+8 merge. Best sub-goal now is take the guaranteed 16 merge while keeping the high tile side stable and preserving empties, so choose left.
